description: Equations, inequalities and sequence basics needed for aptitude.
title: Algebra (Linear & Quadratic equations, Inequalities)

tips.text: Check for common factors before factoring quadratics
Use the discriminant to quickly assess root types

description: Linear equations, quadratic equations, inequalities, sequences and series, exponentials, logarithms and problem solving.
content:
# Linear Algebra Notes for Aptitude & Placement Preparation

## 1. Vectors

### Definition

A vector is an ordered collection of numbers.

Example:

[
\mathbf{v} = \begin{bmatrix} 2 \ 3 \end{bmatrix}
]

### Vector Operations

#### Addition

[
\mathbf{a} + \mathbf{b}
=======================

\begin{bmatrix}
a_1+b_1 \
a_2+b_2
\end{bmatrix}
]

#### Scalar Multiplication

[
k\mathbf{a}
===========

\begin{bmatrix}
ka_1 \
ka_2
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Dot Product

[
\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}
=========================

a_1b_1+a_2b_2+\cdots+a_nb_n
]

### Magnitude

[
|\mathbf{a}|=
\sqrt{a_1^2+a_2^2+\cdots+a_n^2}
]

### Angle Between Vectors

[
\cos\theta=
\frac{\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}}
{|\mathbf{a}||\mathbf{b}|}
]

---

## 2. Matrices

### Matrix Representation

[
A=
\begin{bmatrix}
a_{11} & a_{12}\
a_{21} & a_{22}
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Matrix Addition

[
A+B=[a_{ij}+b_{ij}]
]

### Matrix Multiplication

If A is m×n and B is n×p:

[
C=A\times B
]

[
c_{ij}
======

\sum_{k=1}^{n}
a_{ik}b_{kj}
]

### Transpose

[
(A^T)*{ij}=A*{ji}
]

### Identity Matrix

[
I=
\begin{bmatrix}
1&0\
0&1
\end{bmatrix}
]

Property:

[
AI=IA=A
]

---

## 3. Determinants

### 2×2 Determinant

[
\begin{vmatrix}
a & b\
c & d
\end{vmatrix}
=============

ad-bc
]

### 3×3 Determinant

[
\begin{vmatrix}
a&b&c\
d&e&f\
g&h&i
\end{vmatrix}
=============

a(ei-fh)-b(di-fg)+c(dh-eg)
]

### Properties

* Interchanging two rows changes sign.
* Identical rows ⇒ determinant = 0.
* Determinant of triangular matrix = product of diagonal entries.

---

## 4. Matrix Inverse

For

[
A=
\begin{bmatrix}
a & b\
c & d
\end{bmatrix}
]

[
A^{-1}
======

\frac{1}{ad-bc}
\begin{bmatrix}
d & -b\
-c & a
\end{bmatrix}
]

Condition:

[
ad-bc\neq0
]

---

## 5. Rank of a Matrix

### Definition

Rank = Maximum number of linearly independent rows or columns.

### Important Facts

* Rank ≤ min(rows, columns)
* Full rank matrix has maximum possible rank.
* Used in solving systems of equations.

---

## 6. System of Linear Equations

### Matrix Form

[
AX=B
]

Example:

[
\begin{cases}
2x+y=5\
x-y=1
\end{cases}
]

### Solutions

1. Unique Solution
2. Infinite Solutions
3. No Solution

---

## 7. Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors

### Formula

[
A\mathbf{x}
===========

\lambda \mathbf{x}
]

where

* λ = Eigenvalue
* x = Eigenvector

### Characteristic Equation

[
|A-\lambda I|=0
]

### Importance

* Machine Learning
* PCA
* Computer Graphics
* Data Science Interviews

---

## 8. Linear Independence

Vectors are linearly independent if

[
c_1v_1+c_2v_2+\cdots+c_nv_n=0
]

implies

[
c_1=c_2=\cdots=c_n=0
]

---

## 9. Orthogonality

Two vectors are orthogonal if

[
a\cdot b=0
]

Example:

[
(1,2)\cdot(2,-1)=0
]

---

## 10. Common Placement Formulas

### Dot Product

[
a\cdot b=\sum a_ib_i
]

### Magnitude

[
|a|=\sqrt{\sum a_i^2}
]

### Determinant (2×2)

[
ad-bc
]

### Inverse (2×2)

[
A^{-1}
======

\frac{1}{ad-bc}
\begin{bmatrix}
d&-b\
-c&a
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Eigenvalue Equation

[
|A-\lambda I|=0
]

---

# Practice Questions

## Easy

### Q1

Find:

[
\begin{bmatrix}
1&2\
3&4
\end{bmatrix}
+
\begin{bmatrix}
5&6\
7&8
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Q2

Find determinant:

[
\begin{bmatrix}
2&3\
1&4
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Q3

Find dot product of:

[
(2,3,1)
]

and

[
(1,0,4)
]

### Q4

Find magnitude of vector:

[
(3,4)
]

### Q5

Check whether vectors

[
(1,2)
]

and

[
(2,4)
]

are linearly independent.

---

## Medium

### Q6

Find inverse of

[
\begin{bmatrix}
2&1\
5&3
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Q7

Solve:

[
2x+y=7
]

[
x-y=2
]

### Q8

Find determinant:

[
\begin{bmatrix}
1&2&3\
0&1&4\
5&6&0
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Q9

Find transpose of

[
\begin{bmatrix}
1&2&3\
4&5&6
\end{bmatrix}
]

### Q10

Find rank:

[
\begin{bmatrix}
1&2\
2&4
\end{bmatrix}
]

---

## Interview-Level Questions

### Q11

What is the difference between rank and determinant?

### Q12

Can a singular matrix have an inverse?

### Q13

Why are eigenvalues important in Machine Learning?

### Q14

What is the geometric meaning of a determinant?

### Q15

What is the difference between orthogonal and orthonormal vectors?

---

# Quick Revision Sheet

1. Determinant 2×2 = ad − bc
2. Inverse exists only if determinant ≠ 0
3. Rank = independent rows/columns
4. Orthogonal vectors ⇒ dot product = 0
5. Eigenvalues from |A−λI| = 0
6. Identity matrix acts like number 1
7. Singular matrix ⇒ determinant = 0
8. Full rank ⇒ maximum independent rows/columns
9. Matrix multiplication is not commutative
10. Linear algebra is heavily used in AI, ML, graphics, and data science.
